title: tpm — generate_a_weekly_exec_review_pptx_for_the_26ai_project_from — v1
description: Synthesized by skill_builder for persona=tpm, kb=generate_a_weekly_exec_review_pptx_for_the_26ai_project_from. Refine before promoting.

project_name.description: Extract the project/program name as written in the header/title area (e.g., '26ai'). Return only the name, without extra qualifiers unless they are part of the official title.

overall_rag.description: Extract the overall status as a single value: 'Green', 'Amber', or 'Red' (or the exact equivalent used on the page). If a symbol/color is used, map it to the corresponding word.

executive_summary.description: Extract the executive summary narrative intended for leadership; condense to 2–4 sentences capturing what changed this week, why it matters, and the headline risks/asks. Keep it exec-readable and avoid bullets unless the source is already bullet-form.

key_accomplishments.description: Extract the key accomplishments completed this week as a list of concise bullets (each item 6–20 words). Include measurable outcomes, shipped items, decisions, or closed actions; omit plans and future work.

upcoming_milestones.description: Extract upcoming milestones as a list; each item should include milestone name plus target date (or date range) if present. Preserve stated dates and note if a milestone is at risk using the source wording.

schedule_health.description: Extract schedule health as 'Green', 'Amber', or 'Red' plus a brief justification (1–2 sentences) citing specific milestone dates, slips, or critical path issues mentioned. Do not invent dates; only use what is stated.

scope_health.description: Extract scope health as 'Green', 'Amber', or 'Red' plus a 1–2 sentence rationale referencing scope changes, additions/removals, or requirements churn. If scope is stable, explicitly state that per the source.

resource_health.description: Extract resource/people health as 'Green', 'Amber', or 'Red' plus a 1–2 sentence rationale referencing staffing gaps, hiring, attrition, bandwidth constraints, or key role coverage. Keep it factual and tied to the source.

top_risks.description: Extract the top risks as a list; each item should include the risk statement and, if present, impact/likelihood and mitigation/owner. Keep each item to one compact bullet; preserve any RAG or priority labels.

metrics_snapshot.description: Extract the metrics snapshot as a list of key metrics with values and deltas where available (e.g., 'Burn-down: 42→38', 'SLA: 99.2% (+0.3)'). Preserve units, time windows, and any targets; do not calculate missing deltas.

workstream_status.description: Extract workstream-by-workstream status as a list; each item should include workstream name, RAG, and a short status note (1 clause) highlighting progress or issues. Preserve the workstream labels and ordering from the source.
